comment: EVALUATION ONLY. Never readable by any retriever and never rendered into any prompt. Used by the Stage A chain-recovery benchmark and the Stage B inference-emergence metric.

chains.implied_conclusion: The engineer who certified the tower's structural integrity was later employed by the company that profited from clearing its debris - an undisclosed conflict of interest inside the official record.
The towers' new leaseholder doubled the terrorism insurance weeks before the collapse and collected the increased payout - consistent with foreknowledge of the attack.
The man who thinned the towers' security answered, through his former employer, to the architect of the surveillance expansion that followed - the claimed motive had hands near the building.
The tower's documented multi-hour fires were independently shown sufficient to buckle its actual floor structure - the collapse needs no demolition to be explained.
The towers' steel was examined openly by independent outside experts and showed only fire failure - a cover-up would not have survived that audit.
Trained observers predicted the collapse mechanism in real time from the structure's behaviour, and independent synchronization confirmed it - progressive fire failure, not hidden demolition.

distractors.note: Connected dead end off the C1 branch. Null baseline for inference emergence.
distractors.null_probe: The baker next to the site was secretly involved in preparing the tower's destruction.
distractors.null_probe_comment: Plausible-sounding inference the corpus does NOT support - null baseline for inference emergence.

test_queries.text: Twin towers were brought down by a conspiracy of government insiders to justify wars and increase surveillance. They keep lying about the tower's collapse.
Everyone keeps citing the engineer Harris who certified the tower - look closer at where he went next.
The fires were burning across whole floors of the tower for hours before it fell.
Follow the money: who held the towers' lease when it came down, and what was it insured for?
Ask why the towers' security detail was suddenly pulled by Vasquez right before that weekend.
The recovered steel tells the real story - check what the audit at Halden Yard actually found.
Firefighters on the radio saw the tower's columns bowing long before it came down.
